sections.heading: First Sector
sections.body:
To begin with, you should be on the far left side of the pit straight as you hurtle towards Turn 1, look for the fifty-meter board, not to brake, but to turn in. This corner can be taken flat out in these modern Formula One cars in seventh gear. Eighth gear is also possible, but the car will start to bog down on the exit.

Once you hit the apex of turn 1, straighten your car up as quickly as you can and drift over to the left-hand side to hug the kerb. Once the kerb disappears, momentum will put you in the middle of the track, drift back to the left and brake between the two marker boards for turn three.

Once you are through turn three, you need to immediately turn your car to the outside of the circuit to give yourself the widest line to cut across the second hairpin of turn four. If you are racing, be careful of divebombs from both players and the AI at this corner.

Straighten your car up and clip the grass on the inside of the following kink, known as turn 5, using half of the astro turf on the outside to carry your speed down the Wellington Straight, finishing sector one as you approach the braking zone of turn six.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake hard, re-charging your ERS in the process after the long straight. For Turn six, take as late an apex as you possibly can to ensure you are as far left as possible for the beginning of Turn seven, Luffield. Turn in smoothly, maintaining your remaining speed, and hug the inside kerb until you can apply power and charge to the left-hand side kerb, being careful not to drop a wheel in the sand and gravel on the exit.

Once you have the power down and you are rowing through the gears, drift right and clip the apex at turn eight, known as Woodcote, whilst being flat out. Power down the old national layout pit straight to Copse corner, infamous for Lewis Hamilton and Max Verstappens big crash in 2021.

Copse Corner

Copse corner is almost flat in these cars, drop to seventh gear and just the slightest tap on the brakes to settle the car at high speed. This will see you through Copse and out towards the Maggots and Becketts complex.

Enter the first section on the right, sweeping left and then dropping from eighth gear to seventh as you avoid the high red sausage kerb at turn eleven. Keep as far right as you can, and make sure to clip the middle apex of turn 12 whilst shifting down another gear to sixth. The upcoming long turns thirteen and fourteen are a prime place for drivers to lose the back end, so take this section carefully to begin with, especially on cold or worn tyres.

Once you have left turn fourteen, you can start making your way over to the left side of the track to set up for Stowe, a seriously high-speed corner in these aerodynamic modern Formula One cars.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Brake just before the fifty-meter board for Stowe corner and change down to sixth or seventh gear, depending on fuel load and DRS engagement. The apex is late, so dont rush it and straighten your car up through the kink left, ready for the final section of Silverstone!

The final section of sector 3, the club chicane

Brake just after the one-hundred-meter board and take a mid-corner apex, immediately, you will have to flick right again and put the power down as quickly as you can without spinning up the rear tyres to go full throttle through the final corner and back over to the left side of the pit straight to setup turn one again.
